question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux.generate sink to emit a null value (if transactionGenerator.next(0) returns null), causing a NullPointerException in the .flatMapIterable(Function.identity()) stage that bypasses the onErrorContinue handlers and terminates the publish Flux?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a .timeout() operator on the overall publish Flux to cause the pipeline to hang indefinitely when all network nodes become unresponsive, preventing the monitor from detecting the outage and triggering any recovery mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the publishMetrics::onSuccess subscriber to be called with a null PublishResponse (if the Mono from transactionPublisher::publish emits null), causing a NullPointerException in the subscriber that terminates the Flux subscription and halts all transaction publishing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between .onErrorContinue() and .parallel() to cause errors to be silently dropped on some parallel rails while being propagated on others, creating an inconsistent state where some publisher threads stop working while others continue, gradually degrading transaction throughput until the network cannot confirm new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Schedulers.single() used in .subscribeOn(Schedulers.single()) to be shared with other components in the Spring application context, causing thread starvation where the publish flow's subscription thread is blocked by another component, preventing the publish pipeline from starting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the publish() bean creates a new Schedulers.newParallel('publisher', ...) on every Spring context refresh (e.g., triggered by a config change), causing old thread pools to leak and new ones to fail to start due to thread limit exhaustion, halting transaction publishing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the .flatMap(Function.identity()) on the resolver parallel stage to have an unbounded concurrency (default Integer.MAX_VALUE), allowing an attacker to flood the pipeline with slow Monos that consume all available memory and crash the JVM, halting all transaction confirmation?
